title: Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer discusses how Machiavelli is widely misunderstood, using his biographical context as a Florentine diplomat to reframe his writings on power and human nature. She draws parallels to modern concerns like Szilard's warning about nuclear weapons, suggesting Machiavelli was similarly motivated by patriotic caution about dangerous knowledge.
writeup: Historian Ada Palmer joins Dwarkesh Patel to reconsider Machiavelli, arguing that the thinker is widely misunderstood. The conversation traces his life, his diplomatic experience, and his study of antiquity, showing how observing European rulers shaped his writings on power and human nature. Palmer frames his work as a warning comparable to Szilard's alarm over nuclear weapons, recasting Machiavelli less as a cynic and more as someone confronting hard realities of politics.
